tasks.subject: [task-execution-safety-guard] [P1] Add a dorkHome-keyed leader lock so only one process fires
tasks.description:
Of N server processes sharing one dorkHome, elect exactly one firing leader; followers register crons (display) but never fire.

FILES:
- NEW: apps/server/src/services/tasks/scheduler-lock.ts
- EDIT: apps/server/src/services/tasks/task-scheduler-service.ts (acquire in start(), heartbeat, release in stop(), isLeader gate in dispatch(); add dorkHome to SchedulerDeps)
- EDIT: apps/server/src/index.ts (pass dorkHome into the scheduler deps — already available at index.ts:441/455)

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Lock file: <dorkHome>/tasks/scheduler.lock, JSON { pid, hostname, startedAt, heartbeatAt }.
- acquire(): if no file OR file.heartbeatAt older than STALE_TTL_MS, write our record atomically (write temp + rename, or O_EXCL create) -> become leader. If a fresh lock is held by another pid -> follower.
- heartbeat: while leader, touch heartbeatAt on an interval HEARTBEAT_MS (10s); STALE_TTL_MS = 30s (3 missed beats) so a crashed leader's lock is steal-able.
- release(): delete the file iff we own it (match pid + startedAt).
- Inject a clock (now: () => number) so tests don't wait real time.
- Wire into SchedulerService: acquire in start() (set this.isLeader); start the heartbeat interval; release + clear interval in stop(). A follower re-attempts acquisition each heartbeat tick so it promotes if the leader dies.
- In dispatch(): early-return when `!this.isLeader` (after the mayFire check from task 1.1; both gates compose).

ACCEPTANCE / TESTS (apps/server/src/services/tasks/__tests__/scheduler-lock.test.ts, temp dorkHome dir, injected clock):
- acquire when file absent -> leader
- follower when a fresh lock held by another pid
- steal when heartbeatAt older than STALE_TTL_MS
- release only when owner (mismatched pid does NOT delete)
- heartbeat advances heartbeatAt
Scheduler integration: two SchedulerService instances on one temp dorkHome -> only the leader fires.
Gate: pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/services/tasks.
tasks.activeForm: Adding the dorkHome-keyed leader lock so only one process fires

tasks.subject: [task-execution-safety-guard] [P1] Add (taskId, scheduledFireTime) dispatch idempotency via a Drizzle dedup table
tasks.description:
Guarantee a given scheduled tick dispatches at most once, even if the env gate and leader lock are both bypassed (lock handoff, follower->leader window, croner double-fire). Manual triggers are EXEMPT.

FILES:
- EDIT: packages/db/src/schema/tasks.ts (new table)
- NEW migration: packages/db/drizzle/0003_*.sql (via `pnpm --filter @dorkos/db db:generate`)
- EDIT: apps/server/src/services/tasks/task-store.ts (insert-if-absent + retention prune)
- EDIT: apps/server/src/services/tasks/task-scheduler-service.ts (derive scheduledFireTime, gate dispatch)

DATA MODEL (packages/db/src/schema/tasks.ts):
```ts
export const pulseDispatchLog = sqliteTable(
  'pulse_dispatch_log',
  {
    taskId: text('task_id').notNull(),
    scheduledFireTime: integer('scheduled_fire_time').notNull(), // epoch ms of the cron tick
    dispatchedAt: integer('dispatched_at').notNull(),
  },
  (t) => ({ uniq: uniqueIndex('pulse_dispatch_log_task_tick').on(t.taskId, t.scheduledFireTime) }),
);
```
Generate migration: `pnpm --filter @dorkos/db db:generate` -> packages/db/drizzle/0003_*.sql; runMigrations(db) applies it on boot.

GATE (dispatch):
1. Register the croner callback as `(self) => this.dispatch(task, self.currentRun())` and thread the scheduled Date (epoch ms) through. If currentRun() is null, fall back to a quantized nextRun(prev) — NEVER wall-clock-at-dispatch (defeats cross-process dedup).
2. Add TaskStore.tryClaimDispatch(taskId, scheduledFireTime): INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING via Drizzle .onConflictDoNothing(); return whether a row was inserted (better-sqlite3 result.changes === 1).
3. In dispatch(), after the mayFire (1.1) and isLeader (2.1) gates and before createRun: if !tryClaimDispatch(...) -> log + return (already fired this tick).

RETENTION:
- TaskStore.pruneDispatchLog(ttlMs = 7*24*60*60*1000): delete rows with scheduled_fire_time older than now-ttl. Call it in start() alongside the existing pruneRuns pass.

ACCEPTANCE / TESTS (apps/server/src/services/tasks/__tests__, fresh migrated temp DB):
- first dispatch for (taskId, tick) inserts + proceeds
- second dispatch for same (taskId, tick) is a no-op (conflict)
- different ticks both proceed
- manual trigger path (triggerManualRun) is NOT deduped (no scheduledFireTime key)
- pruneDispatchLog removes rows past the 7-day TTL, keeps fresh rows
Integration: two schedulers on one temp dorkHome+DB sharing a tick -> exactly ONE run (lock + idempotency together).
Gate: pnpm --filter @dorkos/db db:check (migration committed) && pnpm typecheck && pnpm lint && pnpm vitest run apps/server/src/services/tasks.
tasks.activeForm: Adding (taskId, scheduledFireTime) dispatch idempotency via a Drizzle dedup table
